channel.title: Just In
channel.copyright: Copyright 2026, Australian Broadcasting Corporation. All Rights Reserved.

item.title: From buckin' bulls to boxing: Aussie champ's new challenge
item.description: In his 50s, retired bull rider Troy Dunn has taken up boxing because it's "easier" on his body.
item.dc:creator: Liam McNally

media:group.media:description: Dunn spends his days working on his Wagyu cattle property in Bloomsbury, guiding the next generation of bull riders, and boxing.

item.title: Chalmers reveals gas tax revenue is up in federal budget
item.description: Treasurer Jim Chalmers says the tax on offshore gas projects has raised more revenue than expected this year, as he prepares to hand down a budget focused on fixing a "broken" housing market and tax system.
item.dc:creator: Jane Norman

media:group.media:description: Jim Chalmers will deliver the federal budget on Tuesday.

item.title: Students chasing hydrogen racing world title in Switzerland
item.description: A group of Whyalla high school students is heading to Switzerland to race a remote-control hydrogen-powered car that they designed and engineered.
item.dc:creator: Arj Ganesan

media:group.media:description: Five students from Whyalla have earned a start in an international hydrogen-powered model car race.

item.title: Iran fears captain may be denied entry to US for World Cup
item.description: Iran demands assurances that team members who served in the Revolutionary Guard, including skipper Mehdi Taremi, will be able to get visas for the FIFA World Cup.

media:group.media:description: Mehdi Taremi is captain of the Iranian national team.

item.title: Singer Kira Puru was 'immediately humbled' on first acting job
item.description: Kira Puru is known for their triple j Hottest 100-charting energetic pop music, but this year the musician has roles in not one, but two of the biggest Australian TV shows. What's behind the pivot?
item.dc:creator: Yasmin Jeffery

media:group.media:description: Kira Puru.

item.title: Live: Iran's IRGC threatens US sites as Trump awaits Tehran response
item.description: Iran's Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps has threatened to target US sites if its tankers come under fire, Iranian media is reporting. It comes as Washington continues to wait for Tehran's response to its latest negotiating position. Follow live.

media:group.media:description: Iranian missiles are displayed at the Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps(IRGC) Aerospace Force Museum in Tehran, Iran, November 12, 2025. (Majid Asgaripour/WANA via Reuters)

item.title: How a post office in a train carriage became a popular meeting spot
item.description: A train carriage that has been transformed into a Wheatbelt post office is bringing a small community together.
item.dc:creator: Eliza Bidstrup

media:group.media:description: Volunteers brought the carriage post office to life within a month of the town's general store closing.

item.title: Cider mill makes sweet comeback after 100 years
item.description: A recently restored, hand-powered mill that revolutionised cider-making has crushed its first batch of apples in more than 100 years.
item.dc:creator: Justin Huntsdale

media:group.media:description: An original Albert Day cider mill has been restored back to working order after being in the Bradwood museum.

item.title: Ongoing breakdowns hamper breast screening service in Tasmania
item.description: Angie is almost the age her mum was when she died from breast cancer, so getting a screening is important to her. But access to Tasmania's screening service has been difficult.
item.dc:creator: Georgia Hogge

media:group.media:description: Angie Bisset (right) and her mother (left).

item.title: WA teens say anonymous 'alt' accounts used to bypass social media ban
item.description: Teenagers in Western Australia's north are calling for better social media regulation to prevent "alt" accounts from spreading malicious content.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Jackson

media:group.media:description: Broome students say there are multiple ways to get around the social media ban.

item.title: Vine out of Giro after horrific crash, director called 'scared dog' by riders
item.description: Jay Vine's awful luck continued on stage two of the Giro d'Italia with the Australian taken to hospital following a horror crash on wet roads that saw multiple riders abandon the race.
item.dc:creator: Simon Smale

media:group.media:description: Jay Vine is out of this year's race following a sickening crash.

item.title: The 'little plastic ponies' capturing hearts across Australia
item.description: Model horses are a hobby not many are familiar with. But collectors say it should not be reduced to playing with toys.
item.dc:creator: Millie Roberts

media:group.media:description: Model horses collected by Maeve King over the years.

item.title: Regional sports 'stronger through diversity' as refugees thrive on field
item.description: A hockey program in Toowoomba is helping Yazidi refugees adapt to life in Australia and build a community.
item.dc:creator: Grace Nakamura

media:group.media:description: Chinar Ali says hockey has helped her to make new friends and adapt to Australia.

item.title: The 'barrier' that must be removed before landmark Defence inquiry
item.description: The impending military sexual violence inquiry is a time for Defence to "reckon with" its past. But advocates say it will be half-baked unless defence lifts gag orders on victims ahead of hearings.
item.dc:creator: Laura Lavelle

media:group.media:description: The Defence and Veterans' Service Commission is set to lead an inquiry into military sexual violence within the ADF.

item.title: Whale disposal scrutinised after boat ramp 'debacle' attracts sharks
item.description: Marine scientists want to see whale carcasses towed offshore and disposed of out to sea in a process they say is safer, cheaper and more ecologically friendly.
item.dc:creator: Penny Burfitt

media:group.media:description: The operation to remove the whale left the water around the Bellambi Boat Ramp churned and bloodied.

item.title: The invisible problem sitting under Queensland streets and homes
item.description: At least 22,000km of water mains in Queensland's network are headed towards the end of their useful life span, leading to an "infrastructure cliff".
item.dc:creator: Hannah Moon

media:group.media:description: Kristy Reid-Smith says there have been multiple rounds of clean-up after their home flooded.

item.title: Leah Purcell's The Drover's Wife becomes a powerful new opera
item.description: Leah Purcell’s The Drover’s Wife is being transformed into an opera with music by George Palmer and starring soprano Nina Korbe.
item.dc:creator: Ria Andriani

media:group.media:description: Nina Korbe plays Molly Johnson in the titular Drover's Wife, taking over the role from Leah Purcell.

item.title: How Nigel Farage went from outsider to contender for UK PM
item.description: Nigel Farage has his eye on the prime minister's job himself and, if Reform can sustain this level of support at a general election, the keys to Downing Street may fall to him.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Wearmouth

media:group.media:description: Reform UK's Nigel Farage has triumphed in the UK local elections, in results that put him on course to be the country's next prime minister.

item.title: MP threatens to challenge UK PM Starmer after local election defeat
item.description: UK Prime Minister Sir Keir Starmer is facing a new setback to his leadership after a former minister said she would challenge him for the top job.

media:group.media:description: Following massive losses in the UK's local elections, several prominent voices in Prime Minister Sir Keir Starmer's Labour party have called for him to step down.

item.title: Breeding program gives endangered frog with 'baritone' call a fighting chance
item.description: Scientists hope the successful captive breeding and release of the endangered Watson's tree frog in Victoria's far east will help aid the species' survival, after its habitat was decimated during the Black Summer bushfires.
item.dc:creator: Danielle Pope

media:group.media:description: The Watson's tree frog was feared extinct after there were no sightings in the wild for 19 years.

item.title: Women's players sick of being told 'don't bite the hand that feeds you'
item.description: Players from various codes across the country will protest gender-based violence and call for greater accountability from clubs when instances of mistreatment arise.
item.dc:creator: Marnie Vinall

media:group.media:description: Jaydan Thomson left her former club after filing a court protection order against a male player.

item.title: Who is responsible for the problems in Alice Springs town camps?
item.description: Conditions in town camps in Alice Springs are under intense scrutiny, but the residents are caught in a tangled web of bureaucracy with little transparency on the money being spent and who exactly is responsible for what.
item.dc:creator: Dana Morse

media:group.media:description: Harley wants to stay in the community he grew up in and help to improve it for his sons.

item.title: Australian cafe owner looking to protect signature drink from imitators
item.description: Melbourne cafe owner Caleb Cha is looking into how he can legally stop shops in Indonesia from imitating his signature Tiger Bomb coffee.
item.dc:creator: Natasya Salim and Raffa Athallah

media:group.media:description: Melbourne cafe owner Caleb Cha says imitators of his signature Tiger Bomb coffee have prompted him to consider what he can do to protect it.

item.title: 'Easy to get influenced': What cheaper weight-loss drugs means for millions
item.description: India has become one of the first countries to introduce cheaper, generic brands of weight loss drugs like Ozempic and Wegovy, but health professionals warn a lack of regulation and oversight is causing problems.
item.dc:creator: Claire Campbell

media:group.media:description: Pankhuri Pareek is one of an increasing number of Indian women prescribed Mounjaro to lose weight.

item.title: The 'magnificent' mansion that housed movie stars, hippies and refugees
item.description: From post-war Jewish refugees, who arrived with nothing but hope, to the bohemian artists and performers who took over in the 1970s, historic Melbourne mansion Labassa has more stories than rooms.
item.dc:creator: Danielle Bonica

media:group.media:description: Labassa today sits out of view in a quiet cul-de-sac.
